entries.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Producer - The Journal
messages.message: I've spent 15+ years producing across agency, film/video, and content, so building a daily pipeline that turns big stories into tight, on-time deliverables is home turf for me. At Yuga Labs I ran the internal team plus an outside production studio to fully rebuild BAYC.com, keeping cross-functional needs, scope, and deadlines connected instead of scattered (https://www.randallfransen.com/work/bored-ape-yacht-club-website-redesign). I'm good at slotting in mid-stride, holding the team on task, and keeping the vendors and moving parts pointed the same way. Worth a quick call to see if I'd fit The Journal's cadence?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Yuga’s property Bored Ape Yacht Club was still rocking it’s original design and functionality in 2023.

workExampleSelection.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: GTM Operations Process Architect
messages.message: I've spent the last stretch building the operating system before accelerating the work: on a confidential mobile app with a tiny team, the real problem wasn't AI output, it was that output scatters everywhere until nothing connects. I built P.H.R.E.D. (https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred) to fix that: a source-of-truth system, workflow patterns, and structure that makes AI-ready documentation actually delegatable. Redesign before you automate, in my experience. On the scale side, I've run a $14MM HTC retainer and $4MM+ in production budgets, and I'm comfortable working cross-functionally with vendors and senior stakeholders. Worth a conversation about the Central Ops tooling roadmap?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Internal Content Producer
messages.message:
I've spent 15+ years producing content across formats: video, web, interactive, and physical activations, usually jumping into small teams on aggressive timelines. That end-to-end, whatever-the-material-needs approach is where I'm most useful.

For Mozilla's Open What You Want campaign we built a Q4 activation connecting digital and IRL over three months, a Gen Z-coded moment meant to feel honest and shareable rather than marketed at. You can see it here: https://www.randallfransen.com/work/billionaire-blast-off-mozilla

Most of my recent work has also been AI-assisted production, so telling Anthropic's internal stories is a fit I'd love to talk through. Open to a quick call?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Create a Gen Z-coded moment to amplify Open What You Want.

messages.title: Process Strategy and Optimization Manager
messages.message:
I did an Executive Producer contract at Airbnb on the AirCover for Hosts global launch, so the operational reality of scaling programs across the platform is familiar ground. Driving cross-functional work to a deadline, keeping stakeholders with competing objectives pointed the same way, is where I've spent 15+ years, most recently as Production Lead for Base at Coinbase and running a $14MM retainer with a team of 4 at Swift.

One piece that maps closely to the AI-into-operations angle: I built P.H.R.E.D., a source-of-truth system with an orchestration agent that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of scattered (https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred). Worth a conversation about the PSO team?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Project Manager, Editorial Product Integration & Partnerships
messages.message: I've spent 15+ years running digital production where product, partnerships, and regional teams all have a stake and none of them share a calendar. Keeping that many threads connected without dropping one is the part I actually like. At Coinbase I was Production Lead for Base, and I ran the global AirCover launch for Airbnb as EP, both cross-functional efforts with partners and product moving at the same time. More recently I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating system for keeping messy multi-team work in one source of truth (https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred). If you're looking for someone to hold the program together across product and partner activations, can we talk?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Voice of the Customer Program Manager
messages.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
messages.message: The VOC role caught my eye because most of what I do is exactly this: pulling scattered signals into one narrative and making it actually move product decisions. I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating system that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of spread across a dozen docs (https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred). That same instinct, one source of truth feeding fast, actionable insight, is what an insight loop lives or dies on in my experience. Fifteen-plus years leading cross-functional teams means I'm comfortable turning messy input into a POV that leadership and frontline both trust. Worth a conversation about how you're scoping the program?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Program Manager, Human Data
messages.message:
I've spent the last couple years building the connective tissue that keeps AI-assisted work from scattering, most recently designing an orchestration system that ties planning, tasks, QA, and handoffs to a single source of truth (P.H.R.E.D.: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred). Turning messy human input into reliable structure is the part I like most.

Before that I ran end-to-end production programs with vendors and cross-functional teams, including a $14MM retainer at Swift and the AirCover global launch at Airbnb. Bringing order to fast, ambiguous work is where I'm useful.

Would you be open to a short call about the Human Data PGM role?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Senior Partnerships Manager, Crypto
messages.message:
I've spent the last few years deep in crypto production, and the partner-and-integration side is where I've lived. At Coinbase I was Production Lead for Base, and at Yuga Labs I led the BAYC.com redesign, which meant coordinating an external studio, Make Me Pulse, on wallet functionality and cross-functional needs across the Apes universe: https://www.randallfransen.com/work/bored-ape-yacht-club-website-redesign

Managing outside partners toward a product roadmap while keeping compliance and engineering aligned is familiar ground for me. I'd like to talk about how the Robinhood Crypto partnerships seat is set up. Open to a conversation?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Yuga’s property Bored Ape Yacht Club was still rocking it’s original design and functionality in 2023.

workExampleSelection.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Senior Governance, Risk, & Compliance Program Manager
messages.message:
I spent my career running cross-functional programs through a lot of ambiguity: a $14MM HTC retainer at Swift with a small team, and lately building AI-assisted workflows where the hard part isn't output, it's keeping decisions, controls, and handoffs from scattering. That's the piece of your GRC role that catches my eye: maturing controls with AI-enabled processes without losing the humans in the loop.

I'll be square on one thing: I don't hold a CISA or CIPP cert, which I know you list as required. If there's flexibility there, I'd love to talk about how I've built the program muscle around exactly these problems. Worth a conversation?

messages.title: Corporate Events Manager, Expo
messages.message:
I spent 15+ years running digital production for teams at AKQA, Crispin, and Coinbase, and the through-line has always been the same work this Expo role needs: keeping vendors, creatives, and cross-functional stakeholders moving toward one high-quality delivery. At Swift I managed a $14MM HTC retainer with a lean team, which is mostly the job of connecting silos before they become fires. Physical activations sit next to the digital launches I've led (AirBnB's global AirCover among them), but the muscle is identical: scope it, place it, run it, land it clean.

I'd love to hear what the hardest coordination problem on Sessions has been. Worth a call?

messages.title: Associate Creative Director, Copy
messages.message: The seat I'd actually fill here isn't the pen, it's the operation around it: keeping a copy team, designers, motion, and producers moving in the same direction on tight product launches. That's the part I've done for 15+ years. At Coinbase I ran production for Base, and at Yuga I led the BAYC.com redesign (https://www.randallfransen.com/work/bored-ape-yacht-club-website-redesign), both cases of translating dense, technical crypto into something people could actually grasp. If you're building out the Creative org and want a producer who can run the machine that ships the copy, worth a quick call?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Yuga’s property Bored Ape Yacht Club was still rocking it’s original design and functionality in 2023.

messages.title: Legal Vendor Program Manager
messages.message: I've spent 15+ years running vendor relationships, budgets, and reporting for large programs, so the operational core of this role lines up with what I actually do day to day. I managed a $14MM HTC retainer with a team of four at Swift, kept billing and forecasting honest, and held outside partners to the terms we agreed on. At AKQA I ran programs of three to four clients with senior direct reports, which meant building the process and playbooks that let it all scale instead of scatter. The legal-specific tooling would be new ground for me, but vendor management, spend analysis, and cross-functional reporting are home turf. Worth a conversation about the vendor ops side?
